title: Moment-grouped Hunch tool surface for discovery & context fallback
context: Audit round 2 showed agents used only 4/19 Hunch tools; entry points failed at the deciding moment. Task-phrase queries returned empty instead of fallback results. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file); con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Reorganize Hunch tool recommendations by decision moment (orient → design → edit → commit → after); add FTS fallback for `hunch_context` on task phrases; embed SessionStart orientation for new installs; add actionable capture nudge to encourage decision recording.
consequences: Tool discoverability increases; developers/agents know which tool applies when, not trying random ones
Lower friction for new installs via SessionStart scaffolding with light-weight graph orientation
Task-phrase queries now find relevant context instead of failing empty
More decision captures triggered by embedded grilling questions in the capture flow
Moment grouping makes Hunch entry points more trustworthy (less 'pick a tool and hope')
alternatives_rejected: Keep flat tool list; add more docs — discoverability problem remains unsolved
Train agents harder on tool names — doesn't address information architecture issue
Add more tools to the surface — root cause was poor discoverability, not missing tools

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep flat tool list; add more docs — discoverability problem remains unsolved
Train agents harder on tool names — doesn't address information architecture issue
Add more tools to the surface — root cause was poor discoverability, not missing tools
